examples.title: Basic: Even/Odd Classification Using Loop with If-Else
examples.description: Demonstrates basic decision making (if-else) within a for loop to classify numbers as even or odd from 1 to n.
examples.explanation: The program uses a for loop to iterate from 1 to n. Inside the loop, the if-else statement checks each number using the modulus operator (%). If i % 2 equals 0, the number is even; otherwise, it's odd. This combines iteration with decision making.

examples.title: Intermediate: Prime Number Checker with While Loop and Decision Break
examples.description: Uses a while loop with early termination (break) decision making to efficiently check if a number is prime.
examples.explanation: The program first handles edge cases (numbers <= 1). Then it uses a while loop to check divisibility from 2 up to sqrt(num). The critical decision point is the break statement - when a divisor is found, the loop terminates immediately, saving unnecessary iterations. This demonstrates decision-making for optimization.

examples.title: Advanced: Menu-Driven ATM Simulation with Nested Switch and Loop
examples.description: Implements a complete ATM simulation demonstrating nested decision making (switch within while loop) with continue statement for input validation.
examples.explanation: This example demonstrates advanced decision making with multiple constructs: (1) while(true) creates an infinite loop for continuous operation, (2) switch statement handles menu selection, (3) nested if-else inside cases validate input and balance, (4) break exits the switch but loop continues, (5) return statement exits the entire program, (6) continue skips to next iteration for invalid menu choices. This simulates real-world menu-driven applications.
